results.possibleIdentity: Scotty Snyder (born 1912) was an American artist, painter, and photographer active from at least the early 1960s through the 1990s. She is noted for her involvement with Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.), specifically participating in the landmark exhibition 'Some More Beginnings: An Exhibition of Submitted Works Involving Technical Materials and Processes' held at the Museum of Modern Art (MoMA) in New York from November 1968 to February 1969. Based in New Jersey, she exhibited paintings at the Visual Arts Center of New Jersey (formerly the Summit Art Association) as early as 1962 and was a member of the Associated Artists of New Jersey. In addition to her visual art, Snyder was a frequent collaborator and performer in the New York avant-garde scene; she appeared in several films and theater pieces by performance artist Stuart Sherman, including the 1987 portrait film 'Scotty Snyder (All Around the Table)' and the film 'Scotty and Stuart' (1977). She also performed in Richard Foreman's 1981 film 'Strong Medicine' and documented many of Robert Wilson's theatrical productions through extensive photography.
